title: Text editing hook for design canvas with IME support
purpose: Manages an invisible textarea for text input during design editing, handling IME composition, caret blinking, keyboard navigation, and formatting shortcuts.
module_context: Exports a React hook useDesignTextEdit that manages text input state and interactions for editing text nodes in a design canvas.

steps.content: Initialize refs for textarea, composition state, and blink timer.
Subscribe to editing text ID from the design editor store.
Create and configure a hidden textarea element when text editing begins.
Set up event listeners for input, composition, and keyboard events on the textarea.
Attach a mouse down handler to the canvas to focus the textarea when clicked.
Start the caret blinking timer and clean up event listeners on unmount.
Retrieve the current editing text ID from the store.
IF no editing ID exists, return null immediately.
Look up the node in the scene graph using the editing ID.
Return the node if found, otherwise return null.
Access the text editor reference from the editor refs.
Set the caret visibility to true in the text editor.
Clear any existing blink interval timer.
Set a new interval timer to toggle caret visibility.
Request a repaint to update the visual caret state.

methods.name: syncText
methods.normalized_params: (nodeId: string, text: string, runs?: SceneNode['styleRuns']): void

steps.content: Prepare changes object with updated text and style runs.
Update the node in the scene graph with the new text and runs.
Request a render update to reflect the text changes.
Access the text editor reference from the editor refs.
IF no text editor exists, return early.
Get the current selection range from the text editor.
Adjust style runs for deletion and insertion based on the selection.
Insert the text into the text editor.
Sync the updated text and runs to the scene graph.
Access the text editor reference from the editor refs.
IF no text editor exists, return early.
Get the current selection range from the text editor.
Adjust style runs for deletion based on the selection or cursor position.
Delete or backspace text in the text editor based on direction.
Sync the updated text and runs to the scene graph.

methods.name: applyFormatting
methods.normalized_params: (nodeId: string, changes: Partial<SceneNode>, label: string): void

steps.content: Update the node with undo capability using the provided changes.
Rebuild the paragraph in the text editor for the updated node.
Request a render update to reflect the formatting changes.
Set the composition state to true to indicate IME input is active.
Set the composition state to false to indicate IME input has ended.
IF no composition data exists, return early.
Get the current editing node from the store.
IF no editing node exists, return early.
Insert the composition text into the editing node.
Clear the textarea value and reset the caret blink.
IF composition is active or no textarea ref exists, return early.
Get the input text from the textarea.
IF no text exists, return early.
Clear the textarea value.
Get the current editing node from the store.
IF no editing node exists, return early.
Insert the input text into the editing node.
Reset the caret blink after text insertion.
IF composition is active, return early to avoid processing key events.
Access the text editor reference from the editor refs.
Get the current editing node from the store.
IF no text editor or editing node exists, return early.
Determine if the meta key is pressed for modifier combinations.
Process the key based on its type and modifiers.
IF text was changed, request a render update and reset the caret blink.
Prevent default behavior for handled keys to avoid browser interference.
Check if text editing is active and textarea ref exists.
IF both conditions are met, schedule a focus on the textarea in the next animation frame.
